title: Resolving Type Inference for a Generic Function with Type Guards

statement.markdown: Implement a generic function `getMax` that takes an array of numbers and returns the maximum value. The function should handle arrays of different lengths and types. However, the current implementation has a logical bug and produces incorrect results when the input array contains non-numeric values.
statement.inputFormat: Input array of numbers and non-numbers
statement.outputFormat: Maximum value in the array, or a special message if the array is empty
statement.notes: The function should be implemented using TypeScript type guards to handle different types of input arrays.

examples.explanation: The maximum value in the array is 5.
The maximum value in the array is 5, ignoring non-numeric values.

hints.text: Use TypeScript type guards to handle different types of input arrays.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: To resolve the type inference, identify the type of the input array and use type guards to handle different cases.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to use the `instanceof` operator to check if the input array contains only numbers.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use TypeScript type guards to handle different types of input arrays and return the maximum value or a special message if the array is empty.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution correctly handles different types of input arrays and returns the maximum value or a special message if the array is empty.
commonMistakes: Failure to handle different types of input arrays
